chunks.text: At the end of the eighteenth century
France was suffering from a serious financial crisis and famine.
Heavy tax weighed upon the peasant and the city poor
while the gap between them and the king Louis XVI and the queen Marie Antoinette at Versailles was simply too great.
The thought of the Enlightenment philosophers Voltaire and Rousseau had already spread among the educated class.
Liberty, equality, and the dignity of the human person.
These ideal would become the trigger of the revolution.
On July 14, 1789, the citizens of Paris stormed the Bastille prison.
This became the starting point of the French revolution.
In August of the same year
the National assembly adopted the Declaration of the Rights of Man and declared that all people held equal rights.
In 1793, Louis XVI was executed by the guillotine.
France was transformed from a monarchy into a republic.
But in the middle of the revolution
the Jacobins under Robespierre carried out the Reign of Terror
and tens of thousands were executed as enemies.
In 1799, the young soldier Napoleon Bonaparte took power in a coup
and the chaos of the revolution came to an end.
He would become emperor in 1804
but the reform that the revolution brought — equality before the law, freedom of religion
the protection of private property — spread throughout Europe.
" Liberty, Equality, Fraternity.
" The three ideal of the French Revolution still flow at the foundation of every democratic society in the world today.
